paper.title: A Hypothetical Paper About Creator Attention Markets

spans.quote: We use attention to mean the audience's scarce focus on parts of a creative project, following the broad lesson of attention mechanisms in machine learning.
spans.note: Synthetic usage intentionally drifts from the Transformer mechanism.

quoted_definition: Attention is the audience's scarce focus on parts of a creative project.
local_context: The paper uses attention as an economic/social metaphor for audience focus, not as a query-key-value mechanism in a neural model.

definition: Scarce human or network focus allocated to parts of a creative project.
causal_role: Determines which project components receive feedback, sharing, or marketing effort.
assumptions: Visible focus can shape project development.
scope: Creative project coordination and social markets.
measurement_or_use: Operationalized through views, comments, shares, wallet-linked actions, and feedback density.
exclusions: Does not require vector embeddings, query-key scoring, or differentiable weighted value aggregation.
downstream_implications: Attention can become a resource allocated by incentives.
Feedback markets can steer creative evolution.

known_limits: Synthetic fixture designed to produce drift; not a claim about an actual paper.
